docstring:
/**
 * @brief Runs global destructors exactly once at program/shared-object teardown.
 *
 * Compiler-generated finalization routine placed in the `.fini_array` section
 * (the counterpart to `frame_dummy` in `.init_array`). It is invoked
 * automatically by the dynamic loader / C runtime when the module is being
 * unloaded (program exit or `dlclose`).
 *
 * Behavior:
 *  - Guards against re-entry using the file-scope `completed` flag; if a
 *    previous invocation already finished, the function returns immediately.
 *  - If `__cxa_finalize` was resolved (non-NULL), it is called with
 *    `__dso_handle` so that destructors registered via `__cxa_atexit` for
 *    this DSO are run.
 *  - Calls `deregister_tm_clones()` to undo the transactional-memory clone
 *    table registration performed at startup.
 *  - Sets `completed = 1` so subsequent calls become no-ops.
 *
 * @note Has no parameters and no return value; all state is module-global
 *       (`completed`, `__cxa_finalize`, `__dso_handle`).
 * @note Not intended to be called from user code; emitted by the toolchain
 *       and registered automatically via `.fini_array`.
 */
assumptions: The byte flag at ctx+0x4150 is the standard `completed.0` guard variable emitted by GCC for __do_global_dtors_aux; renamed to `completed`.
The pointer at ctx+0x3fd0 compared against a non-zero sentinel is the weak reference to __cxa_finalize; the indirect call `0x11a0(*&[ctx+0x4008])` is __cxa_finalize(__dso_handle).
The argument at ctx+0x4008 is the address of __dso_handle, the standard argument passed to __cxa_finalize in this compiler-generated routine.

Collapsed the two return paths (with/without __cxa_finalize call) into a single linear control flow because both set completed=1 and call deregister_tm_clones() — equivalent behavior, idiomatic for the well-known GCC-emitted source.
Dropped the rbp push/epilogue artefacts as ABI noise.

The compare `flag == 0x3fd000000000` is the optimizer's way of testing the GOT slot for __cxa_finalize against zero (a strength-reduction artefact of the relocation); represented as the standard NULL test.
divergences: [low] other: Original compares the loaded value against the constant 0x3fd000000000 rather than 0. This is the standard pattern produced by GCC for weak symbol checks in PIE/PIC code (the constant is an artifact of the relocation/strength-reduction); semantically equivalent to a NULL check on the weak __cxa_finalize symbol.
[low] dropped_side_effect: Original explicitly preserves rbp; the rewrite omits this ABI/frame artifact. No observable behavior change.

[low] control_flow: The two original return paths (with and without the __cxa_finalize call) are collapsed into a single linear sequence. Both paths perform deregister_tm_clones() and set completed=1, so behavior is preserved.
